Tests the behavior of the `charAt` method with a negative index on a string.

# Purpose
This code is a test assertion that checks the behavior of the `String.prototype.charAt` method when given a negative index. The test verifies that calling `charAt` with an index of `-1` on the string `"abc"` returns an empty string `""`. This behavior is consistent with the JavaScript specification, which states that `charAt` should return an empty string for out-of-bounds indices. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on this aspect of the `charAt` method.
